gap_description: Security management policies exist but have not been reviewed or updated in over 18 months. Risk analysis was last completed in 2023 and does not reflect current system architecture.
remediation_recommendation: Update security management policies and conduct a fresh risk analysis reflecting current ePHI systems and infrastructure. Establish annual review cadence.

gap_description: No formal HIPAA-specific risk analysis has been conducted. General IT risk assessments exist but do not address ePHI confidentiality integrity and availability specifically.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct a HIPAA-specific risk analysis covering all systems that create receive maintain or transmit ePHI. Document threat/vulnerability pairs likelihood impact and current controls.

gap_description: Some security controls are in place but no formal risk management plan exists that tracks identified risks to remediation with assigned owners and target dates.
remediation_recommendation: Develop a formal HIPAA risk management plan with a risk register tracking each identified risk to closure. Assign risk owners and establish quarterly review.

gap_description: Sanction policy is documented and included in the employee handbook. Policy has been acknowledged by all current staff.
remediation_recommendation: Maintain annual re-acknowledgment process. Ensure sanction policy is updated to reflect remote work scenarios.

gap_description: Audit logs are enabled on primary ePHI systems but are not reviewed on a regular schedule. No formal log review procedure exists.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a formal log review procedure with defined frequency (weekly for critical systems). Assign responsibility and document review outcomes.

gap_description: A designated Security Officer has been appointed with documented responsibilities. Contact information is included in the Notice of Privacy Practices.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm Security Officer role is current and responsibilities are documented. Review annually.

gap_description: Access control policies exist but do not consistently enforce least privilege. Several users have broader ePHI access than required for their roles.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct an access rights review across all ePHI systems. Remove excessive access and implement role-based access control aligned to job functions.

gap_description: Supervisory procedures for workforce members with ePHI access exist informally but are not documented in policy.
remediation_recommendation: Document formal authorization and supervision procedures for all roles with ePHI access. Include in onboarding checklist.

gap_description: Workforce clearance procedures are ad hoc. Background checks are conducted for some roles but not consistently applied across all positions with ePHI access.
remediation_recommendation: Standardize workforce clearance procedures. Define which roles require background checks and document the determination process.

gap_description: Termination procedures include same-day access revocation for ePHI systems. HR and IT maintain a shared checklist.
remediation_recommendation: Test termination procedure quarterly with a sample of recent terminations to confirm timely execution.

gap_description: Information access management policies exist but access provisioning is inconsistent across systems. Some systems lack formal access request workflows.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a formal access request and approval workflow for all ePHI systems. Document access decisions and retain for audit.

gap_description: Access authorization decisions are made informally without documented approval. No standardized process for access approval exists.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a documented access authorization process requiring manager and data owner approval for all ePHI access grants.

gap_description: No formal process exists for establishing modifying or revoking user access rights in a documented and auditable manner.
remediation_recommendation: Implement access lifecycle management procedures covering provisioning modification and deprovisioning with documented approvals and audit trail.

gap_description: Security awareness training is conducted annually but does not include HIPAA-specific content. Training completion is tracked but enforcement is inconsistent.
remediation_recommendation: Update annual security training to include HIPAA-specific modules covering PHI handling breach reporting and security safeguards. Enforce completion.

gap_description: Security reminders are sent periodically but are not documented or scheduled.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a scheduled security reminder program (minimum quarterly). Document distribution and content.

gap_description: Anti-malware software is deployed on all endpoints. Definitions are updated automatically. Endpoint detection and response (EDR) is in place.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm EDR coverage extends to all systems that access ePHI. Review EDR alerts monthly.

gap_description: Log-in monitoring is enabled on primary ePHI systems but failed login alerts are not configured or reviewed consistently.
remediation_recommendation: Configure automated alerting for repeated failed login attempts. Assign a reviewer and document the review process.

gap_description: A password policy exists but does not meet current NIST guidance. Password complexity requirements are enforced but password manager adoption is low.
remediation_recommendation: Update password policy to align with NIST SP 800-63B. Enforce MFA as a compensating control. Promote password manager adoption.

gap_description: An incident response procedure exists but does not specifically address ePHI breaches or HIPAA breach notification requirements.
remediation_recommendation: Update incident response plan to include a HIPAA-specific breach identification and notification workflow aligned to the Breach Notification Rule (§164.400-414).

gap_description: A contingency plan exists at a high level but has not been tested. Sub-plans for data backup disaster recovery and emergency operations lack detail.
remediation_recommendation: Develop detailed sub-plans for data backup disaster recovery and emergency mode operations. Conduct an annual tabletop exercise.

gap_description: Automated daily backups of all ePHI systems are in place. Backups are encrypted and stored in a geographically separate location.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct quarterly backup restoration tests and document results. Confirm backup scope includes all ePHI systems.

gap_description: A disaster recovery plan exists but RTO and RPO targets for ePHI systems are not defined. Last DR test was over 24 months ago.
remediation_recommendation: Define RTO and RPO for each ePHI system. Conduct a DR test and document results. Establish annual DR test cadence.

gap_description: No emergency mode operation plan exists specifically for ePHI systems. Business continuity documentation does not address ePHI protection during emergency operations.
remediation_recommendation: Develop an emergency mode operation plan for ePHI systems covering critical processes data access and safeguard maintenance during emergency operations.

gap_description: No formal HIPAA Security Rule evaluation has been conducted. General IT security reviews do not assess compliance with specific HIPAA requirements.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct a formal HIPAA Security Rule evaluation assessing the extent to which current policies and procedures meet regulatory requirements. Repeat annually.

gap_description: Business associate agreements (BAAs) are in place for major vendors but the BAA inventory is incomplete. Several vendors with potential ePHI access lack executed BAAs.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct a full vendor inventory to identify all business associates. Execute BAAs with all business associates and establish a BAA renewal tracking process.

gap_description: Physical access to data center and server rooms is controlled via badge access. Visitor logs are maintained. Facilities are monitored by CCTV.
remediation_recommendation: Review badge access logs quarterly. Confirm server room access list is current and limited to authorized personnel.

gap_description: Facility access procedures for disaster recovery scenarios are documented in the contingency plan.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm contingency access procedures are tested as part of annual DR exercise.

gap_description: A facility security plan is in place covering physical security controls for facilities that house ePHI systems.
remediation_recommendation: Review and update facility security plan annually.

gap_description: Workstation use policies exist but are not consistently enforced. Remote work guidelines for ePHI access are informal.
remediation_recommendation: Update workstation use policy to address remote work scenarios. Enforce clean desk policy and screen lock requirements.

gap_description: Physical workstation security controls are in place including screen privacy filters cable locks and clean desk enforcement.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct annual physical security walkthrough to confirm controls are in place.

gap_description: Device and media control policies exist but media disposal procedures are not consistently followed. No formal media tracking log is maintained.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a device and media tracking log. Enforce documented secure disposal procedures for all media containing ePHI including certificates of destruction.

gap_description: Disposal procedures reference data wiping but do not specify approved methods or require documentation of disposal for ePHI-containing media.
remediation_recommendation: Update disposal procedures to specify NIST SP 800-88 compliant methods. Require certificates of destruction for all physical media disposal.

gap_description: Access controls are implemented on primary ePHI systems but several legacy systems lack role-based access control or rely on shared accounts.
remediation_recommendation: Remediate shared accounts on legacy systems. Implement role-based access control across all ePHI systems. Document access control configurations.

gap_description: Unique user IDs are enforced on most systems but one legacy application uses a shared service account for multiple users.
remediation_recommendation: Eliminate shared accounts used by multiple individuals. Assign unique user IDs to all individuals with ePHI access. Remediate legacy system within 90 days.

gap_description: Emergency access procedures are documented and tested. Break-glass accounts are maintained for critical ePHI systems.
remediation_recommendation: Review emergency access accounts quarterly. Confirm access is logged and alerts are triggered on use.

gap_description: Automatic screen lock is enforced via group policy after 10 minutes of inactivity on all managed devices.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm auto-logoff is also enforced on web-based ePHI applications.

gap_description: ePHI at rest is encrypted using AES-256 via storage-layer encryption on all primary systems.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm encryption coverage extends to backup media and portable storage devices.

gap_description: Audit logging is enabled on primary ePHI systems but logs are not reviewed on a consistent schedule. Log retention policy is undefined.
remediation_recommendation: Define log retention policy (minimum 6 years for HIPAA). Implement formal log review procedures. Centralize logs in a SIEM or log management platform.

gap_description: Integrity controls are in place for ePHI at rest but no mechanism exists to detect unauthorized alteration of ePHI in transit or in application layers.
remediation_recommendation: Implement file integrity monitoring for ePHI repositories. Add checksum validation for ePHI data transfers.

gap_description: Password-based authentication is in place for most ePHI systems. MFA is not consistently enforced across all systems with ePHI access.
remediation_recommendation: Enforce MFA for all workforce members and systems accessing ePHI. Prioritize internet-facing systems and those with high ePHI data volumes.

gap_description: TLS 1.2 or higher is enforced for all ePHI transmitted over networks. Unencrypted protocols are disabled.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct annual TLS configuration review. Confirm TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are disabled across all systems.

gap_description: ePHI in transit is encrypted using TLS. VPN is required for remote access to internal ePHI systems.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm VPN split tunneling is disabled for sessions involving ePHI access.

gap_description: Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP) is posted on the website and provided to patients at first service. NPP is reviewed annually.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm NPP is updated following any material change to privacy practices. Retain acknowledgment records.

gap_description: A process for individual right of access requests exists but response time tracking is inconsistent. One request exceeded the 30-day response window in the past 12 months.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a tracking system for all right of access requests with automated reminders at day 20 and day 28. Document all responses and timelines.

gap_description: An amendment request process is documented but has not been tested and staff are unfamiliar with the procedure.
remediation_recommendation: Conduct staff training on amendment request handling. Test the process with a mock request. Document outcomes.

gap_description: No formal process exists for providing accounting of disclosures. Disclosure tracking is inconsistent and no system is in place to fulfill requests within 60 days.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a disclosure tracking log for all disclosures that require accounting. Develop a procedure for fulfilling accounting requests within 60 days.

gap_description: Minimum necessary policies are documented but implementation is inconsistent. Some workflows share more PHI than necessary with downstream systems.
remediation_recommendation: Audit PHI data flows to identify over-sharing. Update integration specifications to limit PHI fields to those necessary. Document minimum necessary determinations.

gap_description: De-identification procedures are referenced in policy but not formally documented. Expert determination method is used informally without documentation.
remediation_recommendation: Document the de-identification methodology used (safe harbor or expert determination). Retain documentation of expert determinations.

gap_description: General security training is conducted annually but HIPAA-specific privacy training is not included. Role-specific training for high-PHI-access roles is absent.
remediation_recommendation: Develop HIPAA privacy training covering minimum necessary PHI handling individual rights and breach reporting. Deliver role-specific training for clinical and billing staff.

gap_description: Administrative technical and physical safeguards are in place to protect the privacy of PHI as documented in the privacy and security program.
remediation_recommendation: Review safeguard effectiveness annually as part of the HIPAA risk analysis.

gap_description: A formal complaint process is documented and communicated to individuals in the Notice of Privacy Practices. Complaints are tracked and responded to.
remediation_recommendation: Review complaint log quarterly. Report complaint trends to Privacy Officer.

gap_description: A sanction policy for privacy violations is referenced in the employee handbook but sanctions have not been consistently applied for documented privacy violations.
remediation_recommendation: Apply sanctions consistently for all documented privacy violations. Maintain a sanction log. Review sanction policy annually.

gap_description: Privacy policies are documented but version control is inconsistent and the six-year retention requirement is not formally tracked.
remediation_recommendation: Implement a document management system for privacy policies with version control and retention tracking. Confirm six-year retention is enforced.

gap_description: BAAs are in place for primary business associates but the inventory is incomplete. The review and renewal process is not formalized.
remediation_recommendation: Inventory all business associates with PHI access. Confirm BAAs are executed and current. Establish a BAA renewal calendar.

gap_description: Minimum necessary policies and procedures for limiting internal access and disclosure are not formally documented beyond general policy statements.
remediation_recommendation: Develop role-specific minimum necessary access standards for all positions that access PHI. Document and train on determination criteria.

gap_description: Policies for uses and disclosures requiring opportunity to agree or object are documented but staff training on these scenarios is limited.
remediation_recommendation: Develop scenario-based training for staff on when patient agreement or objection is required. Include in annual HIPAA training.

gap_description: A designated Privacy Officer is appointed with documented responsibilities. Contact information is published in the Notice of Privacy Practices.
remediation_recommendation: Confirm Privacy Officer role is current. Conduct annual review of Privacy Officer responsibilities.
